column2: Feature
column5: Notes

column2: Continuous page scroll (all pages stacked browser-style)
column5: Re-rated Medium→Hard (2026-06): the single-page assumption is load-bearing everywhere — `_screen_to_pdf` would become screen→(page,x,y), every gesture/zoom/search/anchor path assumes one current page, and 'current page' drives the notes panel (scroll would constantly commit/restore notes while typing). Several sessions, high regression risk. Prerequisite: page→surface LRU render cache (shared with #23 thumbnails). Cheap alternative shipped as #20. REJECTED (2026-07, not planned): the single-page model is load-bearing and the page-flip UX with centred short pages covers the reading flow well enough; not worth the rework.

column2: Text selection (Alt+drag copies text like a browser)
column5: `Alt+drag` highlights words (word-level via `page.get_text`('words')); `Ctrl+C` copies in reading order with line/paragraph breaks. `Alt+click` opens PDF links. Status was stale — shipped in May. Link-following extended (2026-06) for footnotes/citations/cross-refs: _open_link_at now follows BOTH LINK_GOTO and LINK_NAMED (kind 4 — what LaTeX/hyperref emits for \cite/\ref/cross-refs; PyMuPDF usually resolves the named destination into the same page/to fields, and an unresolved page=-1 name falls back to document.resolve_names() via _resolve_named_dest). It reads link['to'] and calls canvas.follow_goto(page, to_y), which (a) handles SAME-page jumps (the common footnote case — go_to_page used to no-op when idx==current), (b) scrolls the destination point into view via _scroll_to_pdf_y (positions to_y ~18% down the viewport at the current zoom, clamped so it never reveals empty space above the page top) instead of landing at the page top, and (c) pushes the reading location (page, ox, oy, scale) onto canvas._nav_history so nav_back() (Alt+Left, wired in _on_global_key capture phase) returns there. History cleared on load(); on_nav_history(can_back) callback drives a one-time _toast hint ('press Alt+Left to go back') on the first follow per session (window._link_hint_shown). LINK_URI still launches the browser. Tests: TestLinkNavigation (follow records history + scrolls, nav_back restores page/view, same-page footnote round-trip, empty-history no-op, load clears, callback push/pop, Alt+click on a real fitz GOTO link via make_linked_pdf, Alt+click on a NAMED link). README navigation bullet + shortcuts table (Alt+click / Alt+Left).

column2: OCR document before opening
column5: DONE (2026-06): scanned-PDF detection + optional OCR via the external 'ocrmypdf' tool. _pdf_needs_ocr(path) samples the first ~10 pages and flags a doc that carries images but has almost no extractable text. On opening such a PDF, _maybe_offer_ocr shows a toast with an 'Add text layer' button (or, if ocrmypdf isn't installed, a one-time install hint). _ocr_document runs `ocrmypdf --skip-text` in a worker thread with a busy toast, then reopens the searchable result in place (mirrors the PPTX-convert flow). Also a menu action 'Add text layer (OCR)' (_ocr_current) for the current tab. Each path is offered only once per session (_ocr_seen). ocrmypdf is an optdepend (PKGBUILD), not a hard dependency. install.sh can install it too: a --with-ocr flag installs it non-interactively, and otherwise an interactive 'Optional: OCR…' step offers it ONLY when ocrmypdf isn't already on PATH (defaults to no; under -y it's skipped with a hint rather than auto-pulling the heavy Tesseract/Ghostscript stack). Distro-aware (_install_ocr): arch — ocrmypdf is AUR-only (pacman -S fails 'target not found'), so install via paru/yay (ocrmypdf+tesseract-data-eng), falling back to pacman tesseract/ghostscript + pip --user ocrmypdf when no AUR helper is present (verify also checks ~/.local/bin/ocrmypdf); the AUR build runs with PATH=/usr/bin:$PATH so a mise/pyenv/asdf python shim doesn't shadow the system python and break makepkg's `python -m build` for AUR Python deps (e.g. fpdf2); deb=ocrmypdf (bundles eng); rpm=ocrmypdf+tesseract-langpack-eng — the eng langpack matters because it isn't pulled automatically and OCR errors without it. Notes preservation (2026-06 fix): OCR originally reopened the temp output via open_file(), which derived a fresh empty notes sidecar in /tmp and severed the document from its real notes (looked like 'OCR deleted all notes'). Now _apply_ocr_result swaps only the searchable PDF into the SAME session, keeping notes_model, _active_notes_path and the original save target (_path); the OCR input is canvas.save_copy() so current strokes/page edits are baked in too; the doc is marked dirty so the text layer is written to the original file on save. Tests: TestOCR (detect scanned vs text vs blank, offer marks scanned-seen not text, _apply_ocr_result keeps notes + save target). README OCR bullet + install.sh prose updated.

column2: Render markdown in notes panel
column5: Live Typora-style rendering implemented with GtkSource TextTags; `Ctrl+B`/I/E formatting shortcuts added.

column2: Copy error messages to clipboard
column5: Errors show in `Adw.AlertDialog` with a Copy Error button (fixed GTK4 clipboard API).

column2: MX Master thumb button (btn 277) toggles pan mode
column5: Hold-to-pan (2026-06): hold button 10 and move the mouse to pan, release to stop (was a press-toggle via GestureSingle). Detected with EventControllerLegacy — the only layer that reports button-10 press AND release through pointer movement, since gestures cancel the sequence once a drag claims it (`extras/probe_thumb.py`). PyGObject quirk: the signal's event arg marshals as None, so the handler falls back to `controller.get_current_event()`.

column2: Mouse buttons 8/9 navigate previous/next page
column5: Added alongside thumb button work.

column2: Set as default application for PDFs
column5: .desktop file registered via xdg-mime; also registered for text/markdown.

column2: Auto-convert PPTX to PDF on open
column5: LibreOffice runs in background thread; shows Converting toast.

column2: Editable ink annotations (erase after reopen)
column5: Migrated from Poppler to PyMuPDF; strokes saved as PDF ink annotations — individually erasable across sessions.

column2: Add / delete pages
column5: `Ctrl+Shift+N` inserts blank page; `Ctrl+Shift+Del` removes current page.

column2: Unsaved changes dialog
column5: Intercepts close-request and open-file when dirty; Save / Discard / Cancel.

column2: LaTeX formula rendering in notes
column5: Requires matplotlib mathtext → Cairo pixmap → TextBuffer child anchor; manageable but needs TextMark bookkeeping for cursor-show/hide like Typora. REJECTED (2026-07, not planned): inline LaTeX accents/symbols (#79) cover the note-taking need; full mathtext rendering would pull in matplotlib and heavy TextMark bookkeeping for little gain.

column2: Multi-page PDF export / reorder pages
column5: Shipped (2026-06): drag a thumbnail onto another in the page-thumbnail sidebar (#32) to reorder. `PDFCanvas.move_page` reorders the doc via `doc.select`(order) and re-keys all_strokes/_anchors and the undo/redo stacks; `NotesModel.reorder` and `_move_page` re-key notes + the undo timelines. Thumbnail rows carry a `Gtk.DragSource`/`Gtk.DropTarget` (int payload) — intra-app DnD, no portal. Export part already covered by save/#18.

column2: Anchor markers linking notes to PDF spots
column5: `Ctrl+Alt+click` inserts `<!-- anchor:X:Y -->` at notes cursor; numbered circles drawn on canvas. Stored invisibly in the sidecar .md file. Cursor line in notes highlights the corresponding marker on the PDF; clicking a marker jumps the notes cursor to its line.

column2: Ctrl+F text search
column5: SearchEntry in a slide-down Revealer above the canvas. Searches all pages via `fitz.search_for`; highlights all hits in yellow, active match in orange. Next/prev with Enter/↑/↓ buttons; wraps around; navigates across pages automatically.

column2: Inline LaTeX-style formatting in notes
column5: Superscripts (x^2 or x^{ab}), subscripts (`x_1` or x_{ab}), and symbol shortcuts (\sum → Σ, \alpha → α, 30+ symbols). Markers hidden on non-cursor lines; symbols substituted in buffer and restored when cursor enters. rise+scale TextTags, no matplotlib.

column2: Export PDF with notes pages
column5: Export with notes (☰ menu). Source pages get their on-page marks (text boxes, callout boxes, numbered anchor circles). REWORK (2026-06): a notes page now carries ONLY information not already on the page — _export_notes_blocks() drops callout & text-box paragraphs (drawn in place) and empty anchors (only the circle is drawn), and prefixes anchor notes with [N]. GROUPING (2026-06, group=True default in the dialog): short notes from consecutive pages are packed onto shared notes pages, each section headed 'Page N'; flush happens when adding a page's blocks would overflow the running notes page (height via _estimate_notes_height; actual layout by _NotesWriter which hand-wraps with fitz.Font.text_length and page-breaks so long notes flow across pages). group=False keeps one-notes-page-per-page (and 'Include pages with no notes' adds an empty one). _export_pdf_with_notes(..., group=False) default keeps old tests' page counts; UI/share pass group=True. Share-to-phone now serves the EXPORTED version (bake closure: save_copy ink then _export_pdf_with_notes group=True). Tests: empty-anchor omitted, anchor [N] numbering, grouping packs w/ Page refs, callout text NOT duplicated on a notes page (TestCallouts updated), multi-page notes flow.

column2: Undo for erase operations
column5: Operation-based undo stack: `Ctrl+Z` undoes draws and erase drags (one drag = one undo group); also restores strokes loaded from file. Stack re-keyed on page insert/delete.

column2: Scroll past page boundary flips page
column5: Scrolling 3 notches past a visible page edge flips the page (resistance resets on direction reversal). Fitted views re-fit the new page; zoomed views keep zoom/horizontal position and align the new page top (bottom when flipping back). Canvas-initiated page changes now fire `on_page_will_change` so the window commits notes first — also fixes note loss on link jumps and cross-page undo.

column2: Autosave + crash recovery
column5: Snapshot dirty PDF+notes to ~/.local/state/sidemark/autosave/<path-hash>/ every 60s via `canvas.save_copy()`; original never touched until explicit save. On open: Recover / Discard / Not now dialog when a snapshot is newer than the file. Snapshot deleted on save and on discard-changes; pruned after 30 days at startup. Limitation: notes-only mode (.md without PDF) is not autosaved yet.

column2: Callouts: anchor note text rendered on the PDF with an arrow
column5: `<!-- callout:X:Y -->` marker paired with an anchor (working name was 'ship'). `Ctrl+Alt+drag` places anchor at press and callout box at release (dashed preview line during drag); plain `Ctrl+Alt+click` stays anchor-only. Paragraph text rendered in a wrapped box on canvas (PangoCairo, screen-space for crisp text) and in `Ctrl+E` export (height measured on a scratch page — `insert_textbox` silently renders nothing on overflow). Shared `_parse_anchors`() feeds canvas, window highlight logic and export. Drag-to-reposition: both anchors AND callout boxes are now draggable. Callout drag mirrors anchor drag — _draw_callout records each box's screen rect in _callout_boxes, _callout_hit_test finds the box under the cursor, the plain-drag branch starts _callout_moving with a grab offset (box doesn't jump to cursor), and on release on_callout_moved rewrites the paired <!-- callout:X:Y --> marker (first callout after the idx-th anchor, before the next anchor). Hover shows a grab cursor over boxes too.

column2: Outline/TOC sidebar
column5: `doc.get_toc()` → ListBox in a slide-out Revealer left of the split pane; rows indented by level, click navigates, `Ctrl+T` or header button toggles. Button disabled for PDFs without a TOC. Pairs with future page-thumbnail sidebar (#14).

column2: Recent files menu + launcher integration
column5: Shipped: `recent.json` in ~/.local/share/sidemark (newest first, cap 15, scratchpad/blanks excluded); header MenuButton rebuilt on popover show; `Gtk.RecentManager` feeds `recently-used.xbel` (GTK dialogs, KF6 KRecentDocument/krunner). Walker/Omarchy: `extras/sidemark_recent.lua` elephant menu (jq-based, modeled on the local `vscode.lua`), installed via opt-in ./`install.sh` --walker-menu (xbel + default-app handling already covers most workflows); sidemark --list-recent prints name\tpath without importing GTK for other launchers. Skipped: dedicated krunner C++/KPackage plugin (xbel already covers KDE; a native runner would be ~1-2 days incl. packaging). Startup view instead of scratchpad still open.

column2: Highlighter pen mode
column5: Shipped: stroke dict gained 'opacity'; Pen/Highlighter segmented switcher at the top of the pen-settings popover (`Ctrl+H` flips it); while the highlighter is active the header pen button shows a live preview of the highlight stroke instead of the pencil icon. Pen-settings popover (width scale, color button, swatches) routes to whichever tool is active; highlighter defaults to yellow/12pt/0.40. Round-trips through the PDF CA key via `annot.set_opacity` / `annot.opacity` (fitz reports -1 when unset). Eraser, undo/redo and export work unchanged since strokes stay ordinary ink annotations.

column2: Stylus pressure support
column5: `Gtk.GestureStylus` gives pressure, but PDF ink annotations have one width per annotation — variable-width strokes need segmenting into multiple annots. Needs hardware to verify.

column2: Flatpak packaging
column5: Manifest + PyMuPDF module build is the main effort; Flathub would reach non-Arch users. No code changes.

column2: Keep PDF view adjusted when notes sidebar toggles or window resizes
column5: Canvas reacts to its GTK resize signal: while in fitted state (tracked via `_is_fitted`, cleared by any manual zoom/pan) a resize re-fits the page; a manually zoomed view keeps the PDF point at the viewport center anchored. Zoom level is never changed for zoomed views.

column2: Global chronological undo
column5: Single `Ctrl+Z` timeline across canvas and notes (fixes draw→type→draw undoing in the wrong order when focus stayed in the sidebar). Window keeps `_undo_timeline`: one entry per draw/erase gesture (`canvas.on_user_action`) and one per typing burst between two canvas actions (token stores the page and the text before the burst). Capture-phase key controller intercepts `Ctrl+Z` before the TextView (skipped when a `Gtk.Editable` like the search entry has focus). Notes undo restores the snapshot (irreversible `set_text` — buffer-native undo replaced by the timeline) and jumps to the token's page. Timeline re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open. Redo added as #31.

column2: Animate notes sidebar toggle
column5: The notes panel sits in a `Gtk.Paned` (resizable), so the outline's `Gtk.Revealer` slide can't be reused — instead `Adw.TimedAnimation` drives paned `set_position` over 250ms (ease-out-cubic). Box hidden only when the collapse finishes; a 600ms skip-guard finishes the slide when no frames tick (offscreen tests, hidden window).

column2: Redo (Ctrl+Y / Ctrl+Shift+Z)
column5: Mirrors the global undo timeline: canvas keeps a `_redo_stack` of op-lists exactly as `undo_last` popped them (erase groups round-trip); window keeps `_redo_timeline` with notes tokens storing before+after text. Any new user action (gesture or typing burst) clears redo. Same capture-phase key controller, header button next to undo. Both stacks re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open.

column2: Page-thumbnail sidebar when the PDF has no outline
column5: Shipped (2026-06): when `get_toc`() is empty, `Ctrl+T` fills the existing `_toc_list` with 96px `Gdk.MemoryTexture` thumbnails (numbered, SINGLE selection) rendered lazily via a `GLib.idle_add` queue invalidated on document change (~0.5ms/page text, 5-30ms scans, ~110KB each). Row click jumps; page changes select + center-scroll the current thumbnail; page insert/delete repopulates. Toggle now bounces only with no document open. Follow-up shipped: Outline ⇄ Pages switcher atop the sidebar when a TOC exists, and the sidebar narrows to hug the thumbnails in Pages view (230px for outline text). Strokes drawn after opening are not yet reflected in the thumbnails — live refresh plus page-reorder drag (#14) can build on a shared render cache later (also the prerequisite cache for #1 continuous scroll).

column2: Thumb button + scroll wheel zooms instead of panning
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `_on_scroll` routes to the cursor-anchored zoom path while `_thumb_panning` is latched; the thumb pan origin is rebased after each zoom step so the next motion event doesn't jump. Now hold-based together with #6 — hold the thumb button and scroll to zoom, release to stop (was a press-toggle), via the EventControllerLegacy press/release detection documented in #6.

column2: Middle-mouse-button drag pans the PDF
column5: Shipped (2026-06): btn==2 branch in `_on_drag_begin` sets the same _panning state as the `Ctrl+drag` path; drag-update/end pan handling needed no changes.

column2: PageUp/PageDown preserve current zoom level
column5: Shipped (2026-06): PageUp/PageDown and mouse buttons 8/9 route through `window._nav_page` → `canvas._flip_page`, the same path as #20 scroll-past-edge flips: fitted views re-fit, zoomed views keep zoom and align the new page's top (bottom when going back). Bounds-clamped no-op at first/last page.

column2: Install opt-in: register as default handler for PowerPoint files
column5: Shipped (2026-06): ./`install.sh` --register-pptx runs xdg-mime default for both PowerPoint mime types (the .desktop file already declared them). Off by default — most users want an office suite as their .pptx handler.

column2: Hold after drawing snaps stroke to straight line (GoodNotes-style)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `STRAIGHT_HOLD_MS` (500 ms) `GLib.timeout_add` re-armed on every motion in `_on_drag_update`; when the cursor rests, `_snap_to_straight` sets `_straight_mode` and collapses `current_stroke` to [start, cursor]. While snapped, only the endpoint follows the cursor; release commits via the normal stroke path (no annotation format change). `_on_drag_begin`/`_on_drag_end` reset the state and cancel the timer. The existing `_draw` preview (which renders `current_stroke`) shows the line live.

column2: Two-finger pinch-to-zoom
column5: `Gtk.GestureZoom` gives a scale factor delta and handles both touchscreen pinch and touchpad pinch (GDK touchpad gesture events) in GTK4. On begin: record viewport anchor at pinch centroid (`get_bounding_box_center`); on scale-changed: apply zoom centred on that anchor — same math as the existing `Ctrl+scroll` zoom. Main risk: interplay with the EventControllerScroll (two-finger touchpad scroll = pan) on the same canvas; needs claim/deny tuning. Cannot test touchscreen without hardware; touchpad pinch testable locally.

column2: Drag-and-drop a file onto the window to open it
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `Gtk.DropTarget` for `Gdk.FileList` on the window; `_on_file_drop` opens the first dropped .pdf/.pptx/.md via the existing `open_file`() path (handles the unsaved-changes prompt and pptx/md dispatch). Unsupported drops show a toast.

column2: Header bar should stay visible in fullscreen
column5: Shipped (2026-06): root cause was the header sitting in the `set_titlebar`() slot, which GTK4 hides in fullscreen by design (not a Hyprland issue). Fix: window base class moved `Gtk.ApplicationWindow`→`Adw.ApplicationWindow`, header now added via `Adw.ToolbarView.add_top_bar`() with the toast overlay as `set_content`() — top bars stay visible in fullscreen. `set_child`→`set_content`. Regression test fullscreens the window and asserts the header stays mapped and lives under the `Adw.ToolbarView`.

column2: Toggle between draw mode and text-select mode
column5: Shipped (2026-06): canvas gained a `select_mode` flag; the modifier-less branch of `_on_drag_begin` routes to the existing `Alt+drag` text-selection state when it's set, otherwise draws. Header `Gtk.ToggleButton` + `Ctrl+M` flip it; a text cursor on the canvas indicates select mode. `Alt+drag` still selects in either mode; right-drag erase unchanged.

column2: Markdown notes: duplicate line (Ctrl+D) and move line up/down (Alt+↑/↓)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `MarkdownNotesView._on_key` gained `Ctrl+D` (duplicate the current line, or every line the selection spans) and `Alt+↑/↓` (move the current or selected lines), alongside the `Ctrl+B`/I/E wrappers. The line ops rebuild the affected span by splitting on newlines — handling a final line with no trailing newline — and go through the normal changed/dirty path so the global undo timeline (#29) records them as ordinary notes edits.

column2: Ctrl+F also searches the Markdown notes panel
column5: Shipped (2026-06): unified PDF+notes search (user picked Word-style ordering — start on the current page, cycle forward, wrap). `_on_search_changed` commits the open note, collects `fitz.search_for` PDF hits plus `_find_note_matches` (case-insensitive substring on the stored `notes_model` text), and builds one flat list ordered by page (`("pdf",page,rect_idx)` / `("note",page,start,end)`). `_go_to_match` switches page for either kind; for note hits `_select_note_match` runs `_restore_note` so the buffer holds the raw stored text (before rehighlight substitutes \\alpha→α on non-cursor lines) and the model offsets map exactly, then selects + scrolls. Searches the model not the buffer to dodge the symbol-substitution offset skew.

column2: /date slash command inserts today's date in notes
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `MarkdownNotesView._on_key` calls `_expand_snippet` on Space/Enter; it walks back to the whitespace-delimited token before the cursor and, if it is `/date`/`/time`/`/now`, replaces it with `datetime` output (ISO date, `%H:%M`, or both) via the normal dirty/undo path. Glued tokens like `foo/date` are left alone. Returns False so the triggering Space/Enter still inserts.

column2: Responsive header bar — collapse non-essential controls when narrow
column5: Shipped (2026-06): an `Adw.Breakpoint` (`max-width: 820px`) on the `PDFEditorWindow` hides the New, Export, and add/delete-page buttons and reveals a far-right ⋮ overflow `Gtk.MenuButton` whose popover carries the same actions (`_overflow_item` wires each to the existing handler). Core controls — Open, Recent, navigation, undo/redo, pen, select-mode, notes toggle, Save — stay visible at every width per user request (Recent explicitly kept). Tool-grouping popover deferred. The missing-icon fix (`_themed_icon`) landed alongside so the bar renders on KDE/Breeze too.

column2: Drag a placed anchor to reposition it
column5: Shipped (2026-06): completes the "drag-to-reposition still open" note from #22. Pressing an anchor circle starts a drag (`_anchor_dragging`); `_on_drag_update` moves it live by mutating the parsed `_anchors` dict, and `_on_drag_end` rewrites the idx-th `<!-- anchor:X:Y -->` marker in the notes via `_on_anchor_moved` (the buffer "changed" handler re-parses to refresh the canvas). A press under the move threshold (4px) still jumps the notes cursor like before; a grab/grabbing cursor signals the affordance.

column2: Stroke smoothing (clean freehand ink)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `_smooth_points` applies Laplacian (moving-average) smoothing to the polyline on commit in `_on_drag_end` — interior points pulled toward their neighbours' midpoint over 4 passes, endpoints fixed, point count preserved (same ink-annotation format, eraser hit-testing unchanged). Strength is `canvas.smoothing` (0..1, default 0.5), exposed as a Smoothing slider (0-100%) in the pen-settings popover via `_on_smoothing_changed`. Snapped straight lines (#34, via captured `was_straight`) and ≤2-point dots are left untouched. Chose commit-time over live smoothing for simplicity; Catmull-Rom resampling deferred (moving-average already removes jitter).

column2: Lasso select strokes (move / delete / recolor)
column5: Done (2026-06): new 'lasso' tool (distinct from the text-'select' tool #53), wired into _TOOL_ORDER and both tool bars (_mode_lasso/_pmode_lasso) with a cairo dashed-loop+rope-tail glyph (_draw_mode_lasso). Activated by the toolbar button or the discoverable modifier gesture Ctrl+Shift+Alt+drag (added to _modifier_tool so the button lights up; _on_click_pressed's Ctrl+Alt anchor-drop now excludes Shift so the lasso combo doesn't drop an anchor). Drawing a freehand loop selects every stroke on the page with at least one point inside it (forgiving 'any point inside' rule via even-odd ray-cast _point_in_polygon over each stroke's pts in PDF space). Selected strokes get a translucent accent glow + a dashed bounding box (_draw_lasso, drawn after the strokes). Pressing inside the selection bbox grabs it: _on_drag_update translates the selected strokes' pts live from a snapshot (_lasso_move_orig), _on_drag_end records one ('lasso_move', page, refs, dx, dy) undo op. Delete/Backspace (when notes editor unfocused) removes the selection reusing the existing erase op/group machinery (delete_selected_strokes); Escape clears it. Recolor is live via the pen popover: _on_color_changed/_on_width_changed call _recolor_lasso_if_any → canvas.recolor_selected(color,width,opacity) which snapshots before-state into a ('recolor', page, before, nc, nw, no) undo op. undo_last/redo_last extended with lasso_move + recolor branches (stroke refs survive page-reorder re-keying since those only rewrite op[1]); selection is per-page and transient — cleared on _load_page, on leaving the lasso tool (_set_tool_mode), and after any undo/redo. No new deps. Tests: TestLassoSelect (point-in-polygon, any-point-inside select, outside-ignored, delete+undo, recolor+undo+redo, move+undo+redo via a _FakeDrag, new-loop-clears-prior, page-change clears, selection callback, Ctrl+Shift+Alt+drag gesture, _modifier_tool precedence) + 3 window tests (button/popover sync, tool-switch clears selection, recolor via popover). README feature bullet + shortcuts table + tool-switch modifier list updated.

column2: Header redesign: single grouped row with measured progressive collapse
column5: Shipped (2026-06): replaced the #45 `Adw.Breakpoint`/⋮-overflow header with a compact single-row toolbar — file actions (Open/Recent/New/Save/Export/Shortcuts) folded into a ☰ menu, every bar control is now an icon, tool modes are a segmented pen/highlighter/select switch (custom cairo glyphs for the latter two), and the pen settings live behind a colour-swatch `MenuButton`. Responsiveness is measured, not threshold-based: `_calibrate_header` records each collapse level's real content width via `measure()` on two cluster boxes (so the breakpoints self-calibrate to the active theme/icon set), `_measure_controls` reads the window-button width from the live allocation (`compute_bounds`), and `_update_header_collapse` (driven by the canvas `::resize` tick, since `HeaderBar`/`do_size_allocate` don't fire here) picks the least-collapsed level that fits, with 16px hysteresis. Level 1 folds the tool switch into a synced mirror inside the pen popover; level 2 also hides undo/redo/find. The start cluster is wrapped in a non-scrolling `ScrolledWindow` (`propagate-natural-width`) so the icon bar's own minimum width doesn't become the window's floor and block the window from ever getting narrow enough to collapse.

column2: Touchpad: smooth two-finger pan (no axis lock)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): dropped the `DISCRETE` flag from the canvas `Gtk.EventControllerScroll` — it quantised touchpad two-finger scroll into wheel notches that axis-locked to horizontal *or* vertical. `_on_scroll` now branches on `ctrl.get_unit()`: SURFACE (touchpad) deltas pan 1:1 in both axes at once for smooth diagonal panning, while WHEEL (mouse) keeps the 30px-per-notch step. Page-flip resistance is per-source too — `_handle_boundary_flip` takes a threshold (3 notches for the wheel, 180px for the touchpad). A gentle two-finger drag now pans smoothly instead of jittering; a real pinch still routes through `GestureZoom` for combined zoom+pan.

column2: Open multiple PDFs at once (tabs in one window)
column5: DONE (2026-06) — inline tabs in one window, as designed. Each open document is a DocumentSession (sidemark.py) holding its canvas/notes/sidebar/search subtree + all per-document state; the window proxies ~28 per-doc attributes (DocumentSession.STATE+WIDGETS) onto itself via _session_prop so the ~140 existing methods keep acting on the active tab unchanged. _build_document_widgets(s) builds one tab's subtree and wires EVERY signal through s.win (not self) so a torn-off tab retargets by re-pointing s.win. UI: an Adw.TabView (window content) + a real Adw.TabBar as a second Adw.ToolbarView top bar below the header, with set_autohide(True) -> a single document shows no tab chrome (PDF never moves down) and a full-width usable strip appears only with 2+ tabs. (First tried inline in the header title slot per the design, but the button-dense toolbar squeezed the title slot so tabs were clipped to ~half width — the autohide row is the usable form.) Routing (source-decides-target): open_file_in_tab() reuses a pristine scratchpad tab else adds a new one, wired into Open dialog / Ctrl+O / Open recent / drag-onto-window / Ctrl+N; external opens still get their own process. Per-tab lifecycle: Ctrl+W closes the active tab (close-page prompts for unsaved changes; window closes when its last tab goes); window close walks each dirty tab; _autosave_tick snapshots every dirty session; tab switch commits the outgoing tab's note. Tear-off: create-window spawns a fresh window and page-attached adopts the session (s.win repointed + sidebar repopulated + the new window's empty scratchpad pruned) — verified via transfer_page. Tests: TestMultiTab. No new deps (Adw.TabView/TabBar are libadwaita). Cross-PROCESS grouping of two separately-launched windows stays out of scope (NON_UNIQUE) -> #69. Original design notes below. (1) Extract a DocumentSession owning the per-document state currently fused into PDFEditorWindow (canvas, notes_model, _path/_notes_path/_active_notes_path, _dirty, _undo_timeline/_redo_timeline + typing-burst trackers, _search_* state, presenter, drag-export temp dir, the 60s autosave tick); ship this FIRST with single-doc behaviour unchanged + green tests. (2) An Adw.TabView of sessions, with the tab switcher being a real Adw.TabBar reparented into the header's empty title slot (header.set_title_widget(tab_bar)) so extra tabs cost ZERO vertical space (the header row already exists) yet stay fully visible/discoverable; autohide so a single doc shows no tab chrome (pixel-identical to today); fold the tab cluster into the menu when the responsive header collapses (interacts with _collapse_natural/_header_controls). Must use the real Adw.TabBar (not a hand-rolled Gtk.Box) to keep native tab DnD. (3) Source-decides-target routing: opens initiated from WITHIN a window (Open dialog, Ctrl+O, Open recent, drag a PDF onto the window) -> new tab in THAT window; external opens (file manager, CLI) -> new window. NOT a visibility heuristic, NOT a per-open dialog. (4) Tear-off / regroup via native Adw.TabView (in-process, lossless): drag a tab to empty space -> create-window signal spawns a new window with its own TabView; drag a tab onto another window's TabBar -> reparents the page. Ctrl+W closes the active tab (not the window); close-request runs the unsaved prompt per dirty tab; autosave/recovery keyed per session. App stays NON_UNIQUE for now (crash isolation per launch), so cross-PROCESS grouping of two separately-launched windows is out of scope -> see #69.

column2: Modifier-held temporary tool switching (discoverable tool buttons)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): extended the segmented header switch from three tools (pen/highlighter/select) to six — adding pan, zoom-to-region and anchor as first-class tool buttons (custom cairo glyphs: a 4-way move cross, a dashed region rect, a numbered anchor circle). Each tool is the modifier-free shortcut for the matching drag gesture, so `_on_drag_begin`'s plain-drag branch now routes on `canvas.tool` (pan→Ctrl path, zoom→Shift path, anchor→Ctrl+Alt callout path; anchor placement also fires from `_on_click_pressed` on a plain click), while the modifier gestures keep working regardless of tool. Discoverability layer: the canvas key controller (renamed `_on_modifier_key`) tracks Ctrl/Alt/Shift and reports `_modifier_tool()` to the header, which lights the matching button via a transient `.tool-transient` CSS class (accent tint + inset border) without changing the sticky tool — so holding a modifier advertises its binding. Tooltips now spell out the gesture (e.g. 'Pan (Ctrl+drag)', 'Anchor / callout (Ctrl+Alt+click/drag)'). `_set_tool_mode` handles all six, mirrors into the collapsed-popover group, and sets a per-tool cursor (grab/crosshair/text). Builds on #49's switch and the `_syncing_mode` guard. Follow-ups: pan uses a hand-drawn open 'grab' hand glyph and zoom uses the themed `zoom-in-symbolic` magnifier+plus (the conventional cursors); added Ctrl+Shift as a one-off highlighter — Ctrl+Shift+drag lays a single highlighter stroke via a transient `_temp_highlighter` flag read by `_pen_attrs`, reverting on release, and the modifier maps to the highlighter button; and the modifier highlight is now driven by a window-wide CAPTURE-phase key controller so it stays live when the notes editor (not the canvas) holds focus. Icon polish: the pan tool uses the pixel-faithful public-domain Wikimedia pointing-hand cursor (filled outline + cut-out interior in theme fg/bg, auto-centred and scaled in its drawing area); zoom uses the themed `zoom-in-symbolic`. Added an eraser tool (custom tilted-block glyph) — selecting it makes a plain left-drag erase via the same `_erasing`/`_erase_at` path as the always-on right-drag; tooltip 'Eraser (right-drag)'. Pan tooltip enriched to 'Pan (Ctrl+drag · middle-drag · thumb gesture button)' (Logitech's name for the MX Master thumb button). Adwaita has no eraser/highlighter/hand symbol so those stay custom glyphs.

column2: Text selection: reading-order mode + long-press tool menu
column5: Done. select_style ('reading' default | 'rect') on the canvas. Reading-order primitive _words_in_reading_range: caches _ordered_words = _page_words sorted by (block,line,word) on page load, finds the words nearest press & release via _nearest_word_index (point-to-bbox squared distance, 0 if inside) and returns the contiguous slice between them; column-first for free since MuPDF segments columns into separate blocks. _on_drag_update branches on select_style (rect -> _words_in_rect marquee, else reading range); applies to both the Alt+drag gesture and the select tool. Style chooser: long-press either select toggle (bar + popover mirror) opens a Gtk.Popover with two radio CheckButtons kept in sync via _set_select_style / _select_style_radios; tooltip notes the long-press. Long-press only (per decision) since GTK has no native discoverable affordance; tooltip hints at it. Shared word-range primitive + long-press infra ready to reuse for #54. Tests: TestReadingOrderSelection (default, nearest-index, within-line, cross-line reading order, order-independence, style branch) + window test for the style menu.

column2: Highlighter marks text (cheap tier; proper highlight-annot tier as alternative)
column5: Done (cheap tier). highlight_style ('free' default | 'text') on the canvas; long-press either highlighter toggle (bar + popover mirror) opens a Gtk.Popover with Free-hand / Mark-text radios synced via _set_highlight_style / _highlight_style_radios (mirrors #53's select-style infra). In 'text' style a plain left-drag with the highlighter active routes to _text_selecting + _text_highlighting: drag-update reuses #53's _words_in_reading_range (always reading order, ignores the select tool's rect pref); drag-end calls _commit_text_highlight, which groups the selected words by (block,line) and lays one wide ink stroke per line (pts = line x0..x1 at y-mid, width = line height, hl_color/hl_opacity). Stored as ordinary ink, so save (add_ink_annot), eraser and autosave are unchanged. The per-line strokes share a draw group so a single Ctrl+Z removes the whole highlight: undo op tuple extended to ('draw',page,stroke,group) and undo_last/redo_last made group-aware like erase (new _remove_stroke helper). NOT the proper PDF_ANNOT_HIGHLIGHT tier (would need its own storage/draw/save-writer/load/eraser path). Tests: TestTextHighlight (default style, text-vs-free drag routing, one-stroke-per-line, span/width, single-undo group, empty no-op) + window test for the highlight-style menu.

column2: Presentation mode: second-screen live view (no controls/notes)
column5: Done (2026-06): PresenterWindow (Adw.Window) holds a view-only PDFCanvas (new PDFCanvas(interactive=False) param early-returns before adding any input controllers; all state _draw reads is initialised before that return). Black surround for projection. Shares the editor's document/all_strokes/_anchors/n_pages BY REFERENCE, re-pointed on every sync (_repoint) since those objects are swapped out on open / page insert / reorder. Two sync paths keep redraw cheap: _mark_dirty -> presenter.refresh() (repoint + queue_draw only, for live strokes on the current page) and _on_page_changed -> presenter.sync_page() (repoint + _load_page + refit, covers navigation AND structural edits, which all navigate). Keeps its OWN fit-to-page scale/offset (does NOT mirror editor zoom) so you can zoom in to edit a slide while the audience sees it whole. Header toggle (_present_btn, video-display icon) + F5; Esc or window-close untoggles via close-request -> _on_presenter_closed. Monitor pick: Gdk.Display.get_monitors() + get_monitor_at_surface(editor surface); fullscreen_on_monitor the first other monitor, else windowed 960x720 fallback (single screen). Refused with a toast when no document is open. Closed with the editor (_destroy_all / _on_close_request). The toggle button collapses away with undo/redo/find at responsive level 2 (it's in _header_end like the search button; F5 still works while hidden). No new deps. Tests: TestPresenterMode (shared refs + view-only, page-follow, structural-change repoint, toggle-off closes, Esc untoggles button, refused without doc) + level-2 collapse test extended; manual render smoke confirmed 3 frames + live stroke + page-follow.

column2: Text directly on the PDF (callout-only mode; FreeText annot alternative)
column5: DONE (2026-06): shipped the callout-only / standalone text-box mode (the cheap interpretation). New `<!-- textbox:X:Y -->` marker (no anchor) whose paragraph is the box text; _parse_textboxes() parses them and _strip_markers drops the marker. PLACEMENT: Ctrl+Alt+right-click on the canvas (intercepted in _on_drag_begin's button-3 branch; the GestureClick anchor-placer is button-1 only so it doesn't also drop an anchor) -> on_textbox_placed inserts the marker + a selected 'Text' placeholder paragraph at the notes end, reveals the notes panel and focuses it. RENDER: PDFCanvas._draw_text_box reuses the refactored _note_box_layout/_paint_note_box (extracted from _draw_callout) -> same Pango-markup rendering as callouts (#73: symbols/super-subscripts/**md**), but no anchor circle or arrow; drawn before anchors. DRAG: _textbox_hit_test + _textbox_moving mirror the callout drag; on_textbox_moved rewrites the idx-th textbox marker. EXPORT: _draw_export_textbox (callout box minus arrow) in _export_pdf_with_notes. Stacking textboxes<callouts<anchors. NOT the FreeText-annotation tier (still the interoperable-but-bigger alternative; noted for later). Tests: TestTextBox (parse, strip, window place+move, export). README feature bullet + shortcuts (panel + table) updated.

column2: Drag pages from the thumbnail panel to export them (macOS Preview style)
column5: Done — single + multi-page, with notes baked in. The thumbnail row DragSource (_add_thumb_dnd) offers a union content provider (Gdk.ContentProvider.new_union) carrying both the existing reorder int payload (intra-app MOVE) AND a Gdk.FileList GFile (COPY) — actions widened to MOVE|COPY; reorder drop still works (DropTarget only accepts int+MOVE) and a failed export falls back to the reorder-only provider. On prepare (_on_thumb_drag_prepare) the export set is _drag_export_indices(idx): the whole listbox multi-selection when the grabbed row is part of it, else just the grabbed page (Finder/Preview behaviour). PDFCanvas.export_pages(indices, path) bakes current ink (via _write_ink_annotations) then fitz out.insert_pdf per index (sorted+deduped, out-of-range skipped). _export_pages_tempfile then re-keys the dragged pages' notes into a fresh NotesModel and, if any page has notes, runs _export_pdf_with_notes (Ctrl+E layout: notes page after each page that has notes, plus rendered anchors/callouts) — else drops the bare pages; current note is flushed first via _commit_note. Temp files live in a lazily-created per-window dir (_drag_export_dir, mkdtemp) swept by atexit.rmtree; named like Preview via _safe_filename: <base>-pN / -pA-B (contiguous run) / -<n>pages. Multi-page needed the thumbnail list switched from SelectionMode.SINGLE to MULTIPLE; the current-page indicator moved off the listbox selection (which the user now owns for export) onto a .current-page CSS class tracked by _current_thumb_row in _select_thumb. No new deps. Tests: TestPageDragExport (single/multi/dedup/out-of-range/ink export, _safe_filename, drag-prepare offering GdkFileList+gint, and a window test for notes-baking + multi-select + naming); two thumbnail-sidebar tests updated for the CSS current-page marker. Deselection mirrors Preview: a plain (no Ctrl/Shift) click on a thumbnail collapses the multi-selection to that one page (_on_toc_row_activated), and clicking empty sidebar space (_on_toc_list_pressed, a GestureClick on _toc_list) or the main PDF canvas (canvas.on_canvas_press → _clear_thumb_selection, fired from _on_click_pressed) clears it entirely; covered by TestThumbSelectionClearing. README + shortcuts table updated.

column2: PDF shortcuts work regardless of focus + Ctrl+W close
column5: PageUp/PageDown (page flip) and Ctrl+\ (toggle notes) silently failed while the markdown sidebar had focus: the notes TextView's own default bindings consume them before the window's bubble-phase _on_key (PageUp/Down scroll the text, Ctrl+\ deselects). Fix: a capture-phase _on_global_key on the window (same pattern as the Ctrl+Z undo_ctrl and the modifier-tracking mod_ctrl) intercepts just these keys before the focus widget; everything else returns False so typing is untouched. Moved PageUp/Down + Ctrl+\ out of the bubble handler. Other window shortcuts (Ctrl+R/F/H/M/S/O/N/T, Ctrl+Shift+S/N/Del) aren't bound by the TextView so they already bubbled up fine; Ctrl+E stays context-sensitive (inline code in notes, export otherwise). Added Ctrl+W to close the window via self.close() (runs the unsaved-changes prompt) — see #51 for closing only the current tab once multiple PDFs are supported.

column2: Drag-drop a PDF into the sidebar inserts at the drop position, with a live drop indicator
column5: Done. Drop a PDF from a file manager between thumbnails to insert its pages at that gap. Each thumbnail row carries a sync Gtk.DropTarget(int) for intra-app reorder AND a Gtk.DropTargetAsync(Gdk.FileList) for external file insert (async because Wayland file managers transfer through the portal — a sync target's 'drop' never fires; mirrors the window-level _on_drop_async). The async _accept rejects drags carrying the int payload (our own thumbnail drag-out also offers a FileList) so reorder still routes to the sync target. Drop gap = top/bottom half of the hovered row (_gap_for); reorder maps the gap to a move_page dst via _gap_to_dst (removing src first shifts later boundaries). Insert: canvas.insert_pdf_pages(at, path) does doc.insert_pdf(start_at=at) and shifts strokes/anchors/undo (mirrors add_blank_page); window _do_insert_pdfs re-keys notes (NotesModel.shift_for_insert gained a count param) + undo/redo timelines before the canvas navigates. Drop indicator: .drop-before/.drop-after CSS (accent inset edge) on the hovered row via _show/_clear_drop_indicator. Tests: TestPageInsertAndConfirm (notes multi-shift, gap_for/gap_to_dst, canvas insert order+stroke rekey+append-past-end, window note rekey, bad-path no-op, indicator classes).

column2: Confirm page reorder / external-PDF insertion in a dialog before applying
column5: Done. _confirm_page_change(message, on_confirm) gates BOTH reorder and external-PDF insert: shows an Adw.AlertDialog (Cancel/Apply) with a 'Don't ask again' Gtk.CheckButton (set_extra_child); on Apply it runs the change and, if ticked, persists confirm_page_drops=false to ~/.config/sidemark/settings.json (_load_settings/_save_setting, path resolved at call time so tests can redirect via XDG_CONFIG_HOME). When disabled it applies immediately. After applying, a toast reports what happened ('Moved page N -> M' / 'Inserted N pages'), mirroring the save indicator (_toast helper). Tests: confirm-disabled applies immediately, confirm-enabled defers to the dialog.

column2: Text-first mode: type text on the page, anchor drawings to characters so they reflow
column5: DONE (2026-07) as designed; sidemark.py module split SKIPPED on purpose (install.sh/PKGBUILD ship a single file). TextPageView(Gtk.Overlay): MarkdownNotesView styled as white A4-width paper (.text-page CSS, always-light scheme) in a Box inside a ScrolledWindow (the Box keeps the view out of the scrollable protocol, so it grows to full content height = endless paper) + transparent DrawingArea ink overlay whose set_can_target follows the tool (text clicks pass through; pen/highlighter/eraser ink). Strokes anchor to a left-gravity GtkTextMark at the first point + per-point (dx,dy) buffer-px offsets scaled by the notes-font ratio, so ink reflows with edits and font zoom. Session integration: _text_mode flag; _notes_view is repointed at the sheet's editor so search/undo/font-zoom/commit work unchanged; _enter/_leave_text_mode swap paned<->sheet; header hides PDF-only chrome (toc/nav/pages/anchor/lasso/pan/zoom/present/share/notes toggle) including in the responsive collapse levels; the select tool doubles as the Text caret. Open: bare .md (non -notes.md-with-PDF sidecar) opens text-first, upgrading the old notes-only mode; menu New text page / Ctrl+Alt+N (Ctrl+Shift+N was taken by add-page). Storage: the .md is written VERBATIM (byte-identical round-trip, no page markers) + <name>-ink.json sidecar per stroke: line, char offset, 8-hex sha1 of the source line, points, color/width/opacity/font_px; created lazily; on load a stale line index is healed by hash re-match (nearest matching line), else clamped — degraded, never lost. Ink joins the chronological undo timeline as (ink,) ops. Tests: TestTextFirstMode (12). SIDE FIND while building: get_source_text/_wrap/_surround/_restore_line read the buffer without hidden chars, silently stripping rendered markdown markers from every save (data loss) — fixed in its own fix(notes) commit. v2 ideas kept: pagination/print-faithful view, zoom on the sheet, text-snapping highlighter, text-tool margin-drag inks, lasso re-anchoring, export text page to PDF.

column2: Quick-share the current PDF to an Android phone
column5: DONE (2026-06): shipped the dependency-light LOCAL HTTP + QR path. 'Share to phone…' in the ☰ menu (_on_share_to_phone) bakes the current document's ink into a temp copy via canvas.save_copy, then _ShareServer starts a ThreadingHTTPServer bound to 0.0.0.0:0 that serves ONLY that one file under a random secrets.token_urlsafe path (any other path 404s); _lan_ip() (UDP connect trick, no packet sent) builds a reachable URL. _make_qr_png shells out to the optional 'qrencode' tool to render a PNG shown via Gtk.Picture in an Adw.AlertDialog; if qrencode is absent the URL is shown as selectable text. Closing the dialog stops the server and rmtree's the temp dir; a 10-min GLib timeout is a safety net. Both devices must share the LAN. qrencode is an optdepend (PKGBUILD). Tailscale: when a tailnet IP is detected (_tailscale_ip), the dialog shows a SECOND 'Over Tailscale' QR/link (server.url_for(host)); the server already binds 0.0.0.0 so it's reachable on the tailnet IP. This fixes the common failure where the phone is on a Wi-Fi repeater on a different subnet (or AP isolation) and can't reach the wired laptop's LAN IP. RESPONSIVENESS (2026-06): the dialog now opens immediately with a Gtk.Spinner placeholder; _show_share_dialog presents first, then a daemon worker thread runs _share_prepare (save_copy + _ShareServer.start + _make_qr_png) and GLib.idle_add swaps in the QR columns (or an error body). The 'Over Tailscale' column is ALWAYS shown now: a live QR/link when _tailscale_ip() is set, otherwise a network-vpn-symbolic icon + hint (distinguishes 'installed but not connected: tailscale up' from 'not installed: set up Tailscale'). DETECTION FIX (2026-06): `tailscale ip -4` keeps printing the assigned address even after `tailscale down`, which showed a dead QR; _tailscale_ip now parses `tailscale status --json` and only returns an IPv4 when BackendState=='Running' (falls back to the 100.64/10 interface scan only when the CLI is absent/unparseable, which correctly finds nothing while down). LIVE VIEWER (2026-06): the share is no longer a one-time static download — _ShareServer gained a 'live' mode (providers=dict: title/state/render/pdf). It serves a phone viewer page (/{token}/) that shows the CURRENT page as an and polls /{token}/state ({rev,page,pages}) every 1.5s, swapping page.png when rev or page changes — so the phone FOLLOWS ALONG as you draw/flip (teaching use case). /{token}/doc.pdf is a Download button serving the full grouped-notes export. Chose images over an embedded PDF because Android Chrome won't render PDFs inline in an iframe and PDF reloads jump scroll. Thread-safety: HTTP runs on worker threads but fitz/doc belong to the GTK main thread, so render/pdf providers hop to it via _run_on_main (GLib.idle_add + Event, blocks the handler); state is plain int reads (no marshal). Caching: page image keyed by (rev,page), PDF by rev, so polling is cheap and re-render happens only on real change. Window-level _share_revision bumps in _mark_dirty. _render_share_page renders the current page to a ~1500px PNG WITH overlays (ink flattened + text boxes/callouts/anchor circles) by reusing the shared _draw_page_marks() helper (extracted from the export's _draw_marks) on a one-page COPY (insert_pdf into a throwaway doc) so the live document is never polluted; note edits bump the revision via _on_notes_changed->_mark_dirty so the phone refreshes when a callout/textbox changes. _show_share_dialog binds the active canvas so the view keeps following that doc across tab switches. NON-MODAL UI (2026-06): the old Adw.AlertDialog was modal and blocked drawing while sharing — which defeats the live view. Replaced with a NON-MODAL Adw.Window (set_modal(False), transient_for) so you keep editing the PDF behind it; it shows the QR columns + a 'Live' indicator and stops the server on close (10-min safety net closes it too). ENTRY POINT (2026-06): added a qr-code-symbolic header button (self._share_btn) right next to the presenter-view button (mirror to a phone vs to a second screen); hidden at collapse level 2 like _present_btn, with the ☰ 'Share to phone…' item kept as the collapsed fallback. Static single-file mode kept for the older tests. KDE Connect auto-offer NOT implemented (future enhancement). Tests: TestShareToPhone (static token-path+404; live viewer serves html/state/page.png/doc.pdf + 404s; image re-cache only on state change; live url_for -> viewer page; window _render_share_page writes PNG + revision bump; _share_prepare Tailscale hint/link; _tailscale_ip BackendState gating; qr png present/absent). README bullet + menu item.

column2: Bug: first 'Open recent' click does nothing (popover race)
column5: Done (2026-06): root cause was a SIBLING-popover race — Open recent / Keyboard shortcuts each had their own popover on the same menu button, and clicking did menu_pop.popdown() then sibling.popup() in one callback; GTK4 suppresses the second popover while the first is still dismissing, so the first click only closed the menu. A first attempt (deferring the sibling popup() to the menu's 'closed' signal) still failed — 'closed' fires SYNCHRONOUSLY during popdown(), mid-animation/inside the click cycle, so the popup was still suppressed. Real fix: drop the sibling popovers entirely. The ☰ menu popover now holds a single Gtk.Stack (_menu_stack) with 'main'/'recent'/'shortcuts' pages; Open recent and Keyboard shortcuts call _show_menu_page(name) to switch the stack child in place (recent rebuilt first), with a '← title' back button per sub-page returning to 'main'. menu_pop 'show' resets to 'main'. No second popover = no race. Stored self._menu_pop, self._menu_stack, self._recent_menu_item, self._recent_list_box for testability. Test: TestRecentFiles.test_open_recent_switches_menu_stack_in_place.

column2: Customizable tool bindings: right-click / modifier combos assign tools
column5: Make the modifier/right-click -> tool bindings user-customizable. Right-click a tool button in the header switch to bind that tool to the RIGHT-CLICK drag gesture (today right-drag is hardcoded to erase, btn==3 in _on_drag_begin); likewise let the user reassign which tool each modifier combo triggers (today fixed: Ctrl=pan, Alt=select, Shift=zoom, Ctrl+Shift=highlighter, Ctrl+Alt=anchor, Ctrl+Alt+Shift=lasso — hardcoded in BOTH _modifier_tool() and the routing branches of _on_drag_begin). Implementation: a bindings map {gesture_key -> tool} (gesture_key e.g. 'right', 'ctrl', 'alt', 'shift', 'ctrl+shift', ...) persisted in settings.json (_load_settings/_save_setting); _on_drag_begin and _modifier_tool read the map instead of branching on hardcoded combos; the right-click affordance is a GestureClick (button 3) on each tool button that sets that tool as the right-drag binding (maybe a small popover to also pick which modifier combo to bind, since a tool button can't capture a modifier chord by itself). Show the current binding in each tool's tooltip. Builds on #52's _modifier_tool/transient-highlight infra; watch conflicts (two combos -> same tool is fine; one combo -> two tools is not) and keep sensible defaults + a reset.

column2: Ctrl+click a selected sidebar page again to deselect it
column5: Done (2026-06): Ctrl+click on a thumbnail now toggles that page in/out of the multi-page export selection (file-manager/Preview behaviour). Native GtkListBox MULTIPLE-mode toggling was unreliable here because each row also carries a Gtk.DragSource (#57 drag-export) + two DropTargets (#59) that can swallow a stationary Ctrl+click before the listbox updates its selection. Fix: a per-row capture-phase Gtk.GestureClick (_add_thumb_dnd) whose handler _on_thumb_ctrl_pressed, on plain-Ctrl (not Ctrl+Shift), calls _toggle_thumb_selection(row) (row.is_selected() -> unselect_row/select_row) and set_state(CLAIMED) so neither the DragSource nor the native selection also acts on it (no double-toggle, no swallowed click). Plain and Shift clicks fall through to the existing logic untouched (_on_toc_row_activated still collapses to one page on a plain click; row-activated stays a no-op under Ctrl). Tests: TestThumbSelectionClearing.test_ctrl_click_toggles_page_off_and_on (off, back on, toggle-from-empty); existing test_ctrl_click_keeps_selection still valid. README shortcuts table + thumbnails prose updated. No new deps.

column2: Switch the markdown notes to a different file (lazy-create, auto-collapse when empty)
column5: Done (2026-06): three linked behaviours. (1) SWITCH FILE — ☰ menu 'Notes file…' opens a Gtk.FileDialog.save (new or existing .md); _switch_notes_file(new) commits+saves the current notes to their old location first (lazily), then loads the chosen file (NotesModel.load is a no-op if it doesn't exist yet) and refreshes the panel via _restore_note (per user decision: LOAD the chosen file's contents, not redirect). Resolution centralised in _current_notes_path() = _notes_path (notes-only .md mode) else _active_notes_path or notes_path_for(self._path); _active_notes_path is set on open and on switch. (2) LAZY CREATE — _on_save only writes the sidecar when notes_model.has_content() OR the file already exists, so opening+saving a PDF you never annotate no longer conjures an empty <pdf>-notes.md. NotesModel.has_content() = any non-whitespace page note. (3) AUTO-COLLAPSE — _do_open_file calls _set_notes_shown(has_content): a notes-less PDF opens with the panel hidden (toggle synced via handler_block so no animation), springing open on Ctrl+\ or when the user picks a notes file. PERSISTENCE (per user decision: remember per-PDF): the chosen file is stored in settings.json under 'notes_files' {abspath(pdf): notes_path} via _remember_notes_file/_notes_file_for_pdf; reopening the PDF reuses it. Notes-only .md mode unaffected (always shows notes). Tests: TestNotesFileSwitching (has_content, mapping persists, lazy no-file, save-creates-once-written, auto-collapse, panel-shown-when-notes-exist, switch loads+remembers+reopen reuses); TestNotesSidebarAnimation updated to _set_notes_shown(True) first since notes-less PDFs now open collapsed. README Notes section + notes-format note. No new deps.

column2: Keyboard-shortcut tooltips on the sidebar elements
column5: Done (2026-06): the left sidebar's interactive elements now carry shortcut/discoverability tooltips (the header buttons already had them). Thumbnail rows (_populate_thumbnails): 'Page N — click to open (PageUp/PageDown to flip), Ctrl+click to select, drag to reorder or export' (surfaces #65's Ctrl+click and the page-flip keys). Outline rows (_populate_toc): the full (un-ellipsized) section title + 'click to jump (PageUp/PageDown to flip pages)'. The Outline/Pages switcher buttons (_toc_seg_outline/_toc_seg_pages) note 'Ctrl+T toggles this sidebar'. The _toc_btn sidebar toggle already had a Ctrl+T tooltip. The ☰ menu items (Open/Open recent/New/Save/Export with notes/Notes file/Keyboard shortcuts) also gained tooltips with their shortcuts (Ctrl+O/N/S/E) via a tooltip= param on the _menu_item helper. Tests: TestThumbSelectionClearing.test_sidebar_elements_have_shortcut_tooltips, TestOpenAnyFile.test_menu_items_have_tooltips. No new deps.

column2: Open any file as text/Markdown (warn on binary, non-UTF-8, or oversized)
column5: Done (2026-06): the Open dialog (_on_open) now offers an 'All files' filter alongside the documents filter, and _do_open_file routes anything that isn't .pdf/.pptx/.md/.markdown through a text path. _text_open_warning(path) returns a human reason when the file is >5 MB (MAX_TEXT_BYTES), contains NUL bytes (binary), or its first 4 KB isn't valid UTF-8 — otherwise None. With a warning, _confirm_open_as_text shows an Adw.AlertDialog (Cancel / Open anyway, destructive) before loading; clean files open straight away via _open_markdown (notes-only mode). NotesModel.load now reads with errors='replace' (no crash on non-UTF-8) and, when a file has NO <!-- page:N --> markers, keeps the whole content as page-0 notes (also fixes externally authored .md files that previously loaded empty). The PDF body of _do_open_file was extracted into _open_pdf. Saving such a file (Ctrl+S) rewrites it in Sidemark notes format. Tests: TestOpenAnyFile (warning detection, plain-text loads as notes, binary waits for confirmation, menu tooltips) + TestNotes (markerless load as page 0, non-UTF-8 no-raise). README Formats bullet. No new deps.

column2: Cross-process tab grouping (drag a tab between two separately-launched windows)
column5: DONE (2026-07): superseded by #82 — the app became single-instance (HANDLES_COMMAND_LINE), so every launch lands in one process and Adw.TabView's native tab DnD works between any windows. Neither deferred option (temp-file DnD / D-Bus forward) was needed. Original notes: Deferred from #51. Adw.TabView's native tab DnD / tear-off is IN-PROCESS only, and the app is NON_UNIQUE (every CLI / file-manager launch is its own process), so you cannot drag a tab between two cold-launched windows. Two ways to close that gap, both deferred: (a) save-to-temp + file-DnD fallback — the dragged tab offers a Gdk.FileList (a baked PDF + flushed notes written to a temp path) which the OTHER window accepts through its EXISTING external file-drop path (_on_drop_async / the thumbnail DropTargetAsync — the same GdkFileList trick #57 uses to drag pages out); lossy (drops undo/redo history + live dirty state, must pick a canonical path so the receiver doesn't save into /tmp, and needs MOVE semantics: close the source tab only on a successful drop); or (b) go primary-instance (Gio.ApplicationFlags HANDLES_OPEN + do_open, forward the file over D-Bus to the running instance) — clean and removes the need for the temp trick entirely, but one crash takes down all open docs (fights the per-launch crash isolation). Decide once in-window tabs (#51) have shipped.

column2: Command-line --help and shell tab-completion
column5: DONE (2026-06): added -h/--help to BOTH the sidemark CLI and install.sh, plus bash completion for each. sidemark.py prints a _USAGE block and exits BEFORE any GTK import (handled at module scope next to the --list-recent fast path), so `sidemark --help` is instant and works even with a file arg present; documents -h/--help, -v/--verbose, --page N (0-based), --list-recent, and the FILE arg. install.sh gained a _usage() function, -h|--help handling in the arg loop, and rejects unknown -* options with usage+exit 2. Completion: extras/sidemark.bash (completes options; falls back from options to documents via bash-completion's _filedir, or compgen+extglob when sourced standalone) is installed to the bash-completion completions dir by both install.sh (~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/sidemark, removed on --uninstall) and the PKGBUILD (/usr/share/bash-completion/completions/sidemark). extras/install.sh.bash completes install.sh's own flags (source it from the repo; not auto-loaded since install.sh isn't in PATH). Both work in zsh after bashcompinit. Tests: TestCLIHelp (sidemark --help/-h exit 0 + content, help short-circuits before GTK with a file arg, completion scripts pass `bash -n`, install.sh -h). README Command line section + install.sh prose updated. No new deps.

column2: Bug: symbol substitution (\sum, \cdot, …) was persisted to the .md file
column5: DONE (2026-06): the notes editor renders \commands as Unicode symbols (\sum->Σ) by physically substituting non-cursor lines in the GtkTextBuffer (keeping the source in _line_originals, keyed by line). The save/commit path read the buffer directly, so the rendered glyphs leaked into the sidecar .md — opening+closing in Sidemark then viewing in another editor showed Σ instead of \sum. Fix: the NotesModel now stores SOURCE; symbols are display-only. New MarkdownNotesView.get_source_text() reverses the per-line substitutions (using _line_originals with a _symbolize(orig)==current guard) and is used by every persistence/undo read (_commit_note_for, _on_canvas_action burst base, _global_undo after-snapshot). _line_originals keys are now shifted on whole-line insert/delete (insert-text/delete-range handlers) so an already-rendered symbol line never loses its source when lines are added above it; reset_render_state() clears the map on whole-buffer replaces (page switch, undo, notes-only open) to stop cross-page leakage. The symbol table moved to module level (_MD_SYMBOLS/_symbolize) and PDF export now symbolizes for display (the model is source). Added \mapsto->↦. Note: export still can't DRAW Greek/math glyphs (base-14 Helvetica) — same as before, separate font concern. Tests: TestLatexFormatting (get_source_text keeps command after render, plain when nothing rendered, source survives line-inserted-above, reset drops stale originals, \mapsto), TestExportSymbolizes. README notes bullet updated.

column2: Scroll stops at first/last page instead of over-scrolling into empty space
column5: DONE (2026-06): follow-up to #20. Scrolling fast on the last page used to keep panning the page up off-screen (and the first page down) because _handle_boundary_flip returns False on the boundary page, so _on_scroll fell through to the raw offset_y pan with no clamp. Added PDFCanvas._clamp_scroll_offset(), called after the wheel/touchpad pan in _on_scroll: the first page can't scroll below its top and the last page can't rise above its bottom (range [min(0,ch-page_h), max(0,ch-page_h)], applied per-boundary on current_page_idx). Interior pages are untouched (their edges flip), and drag-panning is intentionally left unclamped so you can still pan freely. Tests: TestScrollClamp (last clamps to bottom, first clamps to top, interior unclamped). README page-flip bullet updated.

column2: Render LaTeX symbols / super-subscripts / Markdown in callout boxes
column5: DONE (2026-06): callout boxes on the canvas now render the note paragraph as Pango MARKUP instead of plain text. New module-level _notes_to_pango_markup() does _symbolize (\alpha->α, always — was previously only when the source line was OFF the cursor, since the callout read the live buffer), then ^x/^{..}-><sup>, _x/_{..}-><sub> (previously not rendered at all), then **bold**/*italic*/`code`-><b>/<i>/<tt>; text is GLib.markup_escape_text'd first and scripts run before inline so they nest. _draw_callout calls layout.set_markup(...) with a set_text(_symbolize(...)) fallback on invalid markup. The inline/script regexes moved to module level (_MD_INLINE_RE/_MD_SCRIPT_RE) shared with the editor's TextTag renderer. PDF export already symbolizes callout/notes text but does NOT yet render super/subscripts or bold (fitz insert_textbox has no markup) — follow-up. Groundwork for #56 (callout-only text boxes) which can reuse this markup. Tests: TestCalloutMarkup. README callout bullet updated.

column2: Reopen recently closed tab (Ctrl+Shift+T)
column5: DONE (2026-06): browser-style reopen. The window keeps a bounded _closed_tabs stack of file paths; _remember_closed(s) pushes a tab's path on every real close path in _on_tab_close (clean close, Save, Discard — NOT veto, and NOT tear-off which goes through create-window/page-detached, so moved tabs aren't treated as closed). Ctrl+Shift+T in _on_global_key (capture phase, like Ctrl+W) calls _reopen_closed_tab(), which pops the last still-existing path and open_file_in_tab()s it (notes restore via the per-PDF mapping); empty stack -> toast. Untitled scratch tabs (no path) aren't remembered. Shortcuts help + README updated. Tests: TestMultiTab reopen + empty-stack-safe.

column2: Import PPTX speaker notes into the notes sidebar
column5: DONE (2026-06): when a .pptx is converted to PDF (LibreOffice), its speaker notes are imported into the notes sidebar, one slide -> one page. _extract_pptx_notes(pptx_path) reads the OOXML zip with stdlib zipfile + xml.etree (no python-pptx dep): slide ORDER from ppt/presentation.xml sldIdLst (r:id), rId->slide via presentation.xml.rels, slide->notesSlide via each slide's .rels (_rels/slideN.xml.rels, Type .../notesSlide), then the notes body text from the notesSlide (p:sp txBody a:p/a:t, skipping sldNum/dt/ftr/hdr placeholders). Relationship Targets resolved with posixpath.normpath relative to the part dir. Best-effort: any parse error -> {} so it never blocks opening the deck. _convert_pptx_then_open extracts in the worker thread, then _apply_pptx_notes() sets notes_model per page (out-of-range slides dropped), refreshes the visible note, reveals the panel, marks dirty (saved on first save). Tests: TestPptxNotes (order/skip-empty/skip-placeholder parse, non-pptx -> {}, window apply + out-of-range); synthetic make_pptx() test helper.

column2: Zoomable notes-panel font (Ctrl+± / Ctrl+scroll)
column5: DONE (2026-06): the Markdown notes panel font is now zoomable, mainly so presenter notes are readable from a distance while the projector shows the slide. Ctrl++ / Ctrl+- (and Ctrl+0 to reset) plus Ctrl+scroll over the editor resize it; handled in MarkdownNotesView._on_key / _on_scroll, which delegate to a settable font_zoom_cb wired to the window's _change_notes_font(direction). Size lives in its own display CssProvider (.notes-view font-size) added just above the base sheet so it wins and can be re-loaded at runtime; heading TextTags use scale= so they grow with it. Clamped 9-40px, default 13, persisted globally as notes_font_px in settings.json and reloaded on window open. Shortcuts help + README updated. Tests: TestNotesFontZoom (grow/shrink/reset, clamping, persistence, callback wired).

column2: Presentation timer + large navigation bar on the presenter window
column5: DONE (2026-07): while presentation mode is active, the editor (presenter) window shows a bottom overlay bar with a presentation timer that can be paused and reset, plus large prev/next page buttons. It lives on the presenter's own screen — a Gtk.Overlay wrapping the Adw.TabView (self._present_overlay) — NOT on the projected PresenterWindow, which stays a bare view-only slide for the audience. _build_present_bar() builds it hidden; _open_presenter -> _show_present_bar(True) reveals it and starts a 1s GLib timeout (_present_tick), resetting to 0:00; _close_presenter / _on_presenter_closed hide it and stop the timeout. _toggle_present_timer freezes/resumes, _reset_present_timer zeroes; time formatted by module-level _fmt_clock (m:ss, h:mm:ss past an hour). Nav buttons call _nav_page(±1) so the editor and mirror stay in step. Tests: TestPresenterMode (bar visibility, nav flips both, timer pause/reset, restart-from-zero).

column2: Mouse back/forward (side) buttons flip pages everywhere
column5: DONE (2026-07): the mouse side buttons (back/forward, 8/9) were unreliable for page nav — handled via the canvas drag gesture, which (per the button-10 note) drops extra-button sequences, and only fired when the pointer was over the canvas, never when the notes editor had focus. Moved to a window-level capture-phase EventControllerLegacy (_on_window_button): button 8 -> next, 9 -> prev via _nav_page, returning True to consume; everything else passes through. Works anywhere in the window, including while typing notes. The canvas drag-begin 8/9 branch now only suppresses drawing (_ignoring) and no longer navigates, so no double-flip. Tests: TestMouseSideButtonNav (flips with notes focused, releases/other buttons pass through).

column2: LaTeX accents in notes (\hat \bar \tilde \vec)
column5: DONE (2026-07): \hat{x} \bar{x} \tilde{x} \vec{x} (plus \dot/\ddot) render as the base glyph + a Unicode combining mark (U+0302/0304/0303/20D7/0307/0308), e.g. x-hat. Module-level _apply_accents runs INSIDE _symbolize, after the \command->symbol pass, so the mark lands on an already-substituted glyph (\vec{\alpha} -> alpha with arrow). _MD_ACCENT_RE matches \name{...} or \name<single-char> (braces optional, e.g. \hat x); the mark is inserted after the first character of the argument. Because it's in _symbolize it works everywhere: the notes editor's in-place rendering, callout Pango markup, and PDF export. Tests: accent braced/space/all-kinds/over-greek/in-sentence.

column2: Centre short pages at the document ends instead of top/bottom
column5: DONE (2026-07): when a page is shorter than the viewport (zoomed out, or a wide/landscape slide) there's empty space above and below; fast-scrolling to the start/end used to slam the page against the top or bottom of the window. Now _clamp_scroll_offset pins the first/last page's offset_y to the centred position when page_h <= viewport height (min(.,center) at the start, max(.,center) at the end), and _flip_page centres a short page it lands on rather than aligning to an edge. Zoomed-in pages (taller than the viewport) are unchanged. Tests: TestScrollClamp short-first/short-last centre + flip-to-short centres.

column2: Presenter mode re-fits each slide on next/previous
column5: DONE (2026-07): while the presenter (second-screen) view is open, _nav_page now calls go_to_page(target) (re-fit, keep_view=False) instead of _flip_page, so paging forward/back shows each slide whole and centred rather than carrying a zoomed-in reading position across. Outside presentation mode navigation still keeps the zoom (_flip_page). Tests: TestPresenterMode nav-refits-each-slide and nav-keeps-zoom-when-not-presenting.

column2: Drag a tab between windows (single-instance app)
column5: DONE (2026-07): dragging a tab between windows now works. Root cause: the app was NON_UNIQUE so every launch was its own process, and a live tab (AdwTabPage) can't cross a process boundary — the target instance saw only the application/x-rootwindow-drop marker and its file-drop target then warned 'Drop a PDF…'. Fix: (1) PDFEditorApp is now single-instance via HANDLES_COMMAND_LINE — do_command_line parses each launch's file + --page (resolving relative paths against the invoker's cwd) and open_new_window()s it, so all windows share one process and Adw tab DnD works between any of them; a remote (second) launch prints 'Sidemark is already running — opened X in it.' to the invoking shell via command_line.print_literal. (2) _on_drop_accept declines drags offering AdwTabPage or application/x-rootwindow-drop so the file target no longer swallows a tab drop / warns. (3) Ctrl+R reload spawns its child with SIDEMARK_STANDALONE=1, which adds NON_UNIQUE so the fresh-code process runs independently instead of forwarding. main() passes full argv to app.run and fast-fails a missing file before handing off. Tests: TestSingleInstanceArgs (_parse_open_args), TestDragAndDrop file-target-declines-tab-drags.

column2: Next-slide preview in presentation mode (stack of pages look)
column5: DONE (2026-07): while presentation mode is active, the editor (presenter) canvas draws the pages as a STACK, like PowerPoint presenter view: the current page fits slightly smaller and moves left, and the UPCOMING page — at STACK_NEXT_SCALE (0.45) of the current scale, very slightly greyed (0.10 grey wash) with a hairline — shows behind its right edge, barely overlapping (STACK_OVERLAP 18px) and bottom-anchored to the canvas. Per user decision the next slide's position depends ONLY on the page width (vertical fit stays normal), so portrait pages keep their size/position and nothing jumps between pages. All in PDFCanvas: stack_preview flag + set_stack_preview() (refits when fitted), _fit_page reserves width for the pair, _draw_stack_peek(ctx, height) renders page current+1 via the new _page_to_surface() helper (factored out of _rerender_now), invalidated in _load_page and re-rendered when the scale changes; skipped on the last page. Toggled from _show_present_bar. UPDATE (2026-07): _fit_page now computes BOTH layouts — next slide beside (s_right) or underneath (s_below, e.g. when a wide notes panel leaves the PDF panel tall) — and picks whichever leaves the current page larger (_stack_below flag); _draw_stack_peek(ctx, width, height) anchors the below variant to the canvas right edge, under the page's bottom edge. Test: stack-preview-moves-below-on-tall-canvas. First iteration was a floating corner Gtk.Picture overlay — replaced by this per user feedback. Gotcha: _open_presenter must assign self._presenter BEFORE _show_present_bar(True). Tests: TestPresenterMode stack fit geometry + pixel-level render (neutral greyed paper vs warm surround on last page). README updated.

column2: Navigation controls on the projected presenter window
column5: DONE (2026-07): the projected (audience) PresenterWindow can now drive the presentation when focused — useful with a clicker or when the presenter machine's input goes to that screen. Keys (_on_key): Space/Right/Down/PageDown/KP_PageDown/Return -> next, Left/Up/BackSpace/PageUp/KP_PageUp -> prev, Esc still closes. Mouse (Gtk.GestureClick with set_button(0) on the canvas, _on_click): left click / mouse-forward (8) -> next, right click / mouse-back (9) -> prev (same side-button mapping as the editor, #78). Navigation flows through a new on_nav callback wired to the editor window's _nav_page, so editor + mirror + notes stay in step and slides re-fit (#81). Tests: TestPresenterMode keys-navigate + click-navigates (stub gesture via types.SimpleNamespace).

column2: Live in-progress ink on the presented slide
column5: DONE (2026-07): while presenting, ink now appears on the projected slide AS IT IS DRAWN, not only when the stroke is finished. Mechanism: the mirror canvas sets live_stroke_src = editor canvas and _draw appends the source's current_stroke (with src._pen_attrs() so highlighter width/opacity mirror correctly) when both are on the same page; the editor canvas calls a new on_live_draw callback (wired in PresenterWindow.__init__ to the mirror's queue_draw) on every stroke motion in _on_drag_update, on the straight-line snap (_snap_to_straight), and once more when the stroke commits/clears in _on_drag_end (so no ghost stroke lingers). Live ERASE already mirrored (each _erase_at removal fires on_change -> presenter.refresh). PresenterWindow.detach() unhooks on_live_draw on every close path (_close_presenter calls it; self-close via close-request -> _on_presenter_closed). Test: TestPresenterMode live-stroke-mirrors (pixel-level: blue in-progress ink appears on the mirror surface; callback unhooked after closing). Pen color pinned in the test — the window loads the user's pen color from settings.

column2: Presentation bar restyle: OSD look + scales with the window
column5: DONE (2026-07): the presentation control bar (timer + prev/next) was a fixed-size hard black pill; it now blends with the design while staying clearly visible. (1) SCALES WITH THE WINDOW: all sizes (font, button min-sizes, icon sizes, padding, radius, bottom margin) derive from factor f = clamp(min(w/1280, h/800), 0.8, 2.5), applied via a PER-WINDOW Gtk.CssProvider (self._present_css) whose selectors are scoped by a window-unique css class (present-bar-<id>) so two windows never restyle each other; regenerated from do_size_allocate (override on PDFEditorWindow, chains up; only while the bar is visible, with a 0.04 jitter threshold) and caught up in _show_present_bar. (2) OSD LOOK: softer translucent near-black rgba(15,15,18,0.62), 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.12) hairline border, pill radius, FLAT white icon buttons that show a THEME-ACCENT tint on hover/active (alpha(acc,0.50)/(0.75)) — ties the bar into the pywal/theme accent used everywhere else. Static .present-bar CSS block removed from the main provider. GOTCHA: border-radius: 9999px silently renders square in GTK4 — use computed radii (34f/26f px). Note: headless-weston snapshots render radii square anyway (cairo software renderer artifact), not a real bug. Tests: TestPresenterMode present-bar-scales-with-window (baseline/double/clamps/jitter).

column2: Typing a bracket around a selection surrounds it in the notes editor
column5: DONE (2026-07): in the Markdown notes editor, typing a bracket or quote while text is selected SURROUNDS the selection instead of replacing it — editor-standard behaviour (VSCode etc.). MarkdownNotesView._on_key (no-modifier branch, before slash snippets) maps the pressed key via Gdk.keyval_to_unicode into the class-level _SURROUND_CHARS table: ( ) [ ] { } < > each map (both halves) to their (open, close) pair; " ' ` wrap symmetrically. _surround_selection(opener, closer) wraps in one undoable user action and RE-SELECTS the inner text so pairs can be stacked (( then [ gives ([word])).
